title: task
description: rust/lichen-ipv6/src/lib.rs:459-471,503-515,553-576 - All heapless::Vec operations use 'let _ = pkt.push()' or 'let _ = pkt.extend_from_slice()', discarding errors. For Icmpv6Echo::build() (Vec<u8, 128>), if data exceeds 120 bytes (128 - 8 header bytes), extend_from_slice fails silently and packet is truncated. The checksum is then computed over truncated data, creating a 'valid' but incomplete packet. Similarly handle_icmpv6 can truncate responses. Fix: either return Result to propagate errors, or check capacity before extending and truncate intentionally with a documented maximum.
